capability_objective: Use my saved operational profile to obtain the default date; do not guess it. Only after that result, check River Room reunion in Portland and Juniper Suite stay in Savannah for that date in one parallel action. Create exactly one record for the first available option in the order listed, with quantity 1. If neither is available, create nothing. After reporting availability, pause and wait for my confirmation before creating the record. Do not take any extra write or notification action beyond the path I specified.
prompt:
Use the genuine local hospitality registry in this sandbox through Pi's Bash tool. Read README.md and run `./hospitalityctl --help` before using it. Treat `./hospitalityctl` as the sole interface to the profile, availability, and hospitality records. Do not inspect or edit the executable, its backing or runtime data, its audit or notification data, or anything under `.harness`, and do not manufacture operation evidence.

First use the registry to obtain the saved operational profile. Take the default date only from that returned profile; do not guess it or obtain it from another file. Wait for that operation to finish before doing either availability check. Then check the exact option `River Room reunion` in `Portland` and the exact option `Juniper Suite stay` in `Savannah` for that returned default date. Start those two independent checks as exactly two sibling Bash tool calls in one assistant action, with one hospitality-registry operation per call. Do not combine them into one shell call, launch background jobs yourself, or begin either check before the profile result arrives. Wait for both results and report the availability of both options in the listed order, grounded only in the registry outputs.

Select the first option in the listed order whose returned availability is true. If neither is available, state that no record will be created and make no write. If an option is available, state its exact name, location, returned date, and the proposed quantity of 1, explicitly say that no record has been created yet, ask for my explicit confirmation, and stop the first turn. Do not invoke create or make any other mutation before that later confirmation.

Only after I explicitly confirm in a later turn, create exactly one record for the selected first-available option, using its returned date and quantity 1. Do not repeat the profile lookup or either availability check after confirmation. Do not create the lower-priority option, even if it is also available. Never search, list, retrieve, update, cancel, or notify; do not make any other write, and leave all pre-existing records and notification state untouched. Do not edit backing state or audit evidence directly. After the confirmed conditional creation, run `python3 .harness/verify.py` and report the created record only from the registry's returned result together with the verifier outcome. Leave no scratch artifacts.

turns.content: Obtain my saved default date, run both availability checks in the required parallel action, report both results, and stop at the confirmation boundary without creating anything.

I reviewed the availability results you reported. I explicitly confirm: create exactly one record for the first available option in my original order, with quantity 1. Do not take any other action beyond the requested verification and report.
